title: 6 Processing PADs
body_text:
6	Processing PADs
6.1	Pre-authorized Debit Types
CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, defines four types of Pre-authorized Debits:
Business PAD
A Pre-authorized Debit used to pay for goods or services related to business or commercial activity. This includes payments between franchisees and franchisors, distributors and suppliers, and dealers and manufacturers.
Cash Management PAD
A Pre-authorized Debit used by a business or closely affiliated business to transfer, consolidate, or reposition funds between its accounts at different financial institutions.
Funds Transfer PAD
A Pre-authorized Debit used by individuals to transfer money between accounts they hold at different financial institutions. The Payee and Payor are the same person. Examples include contributions to registered savings plans, mutual funds deposit accounts, and investment accounts.
Personal PAD
A Pre-authorized Debit used by consumers to pay for goods and services such as mortgage payments, insurance premiums, utility bills, membership fees, loans, credit card billings, charitable donations, and investment contributions to companies that are not members of the CPA.
6.2	Providing Notification of PADs
Unless pre-notification is waived under the terms of the Payorâs PAD Agreement or a separate waiver, the Originator must notify the Payor of fixed amount PADs at least ten calendar days before the first PAD, before an annual top-up or adjustment and every time there is a change in the amount or payment date. For variable amount PADs, the Payor must be notified at least ten calendar days before each transaction unless this requirement is waived. Notification in either situation is not required if the Payor directly instructs the Originator to change the amount of the PAD, and the Payorâs PAD Agreement allows for this.
A record of the authorization, either in writing or a recorded message, must be retained for at least 12 months after the PAD to which it applies is terminated. Other legal requirements for document retention can also apply. See CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits.
6.3	Sporadic PADs
Where a PAD is sporadic (e.g., one time; or ongoing but not made according to a fixed schedule), the Originator must obtain authorization from the Payor for each PAD transaction. The Payorâs PAD Agreement must also indicate that the transactions are sporadic. Recording a telephone call in which the Payor authorizes the PAD with a password or secret code could serve this purpose. See CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits.
A record of the authorization must be retained for at least 12 months after the PAD to which it applies. Legal requirements for document retention can also apply. See CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits.
END
